talk_title: Lipid-associated macrophages as therapeutic targets and CAR‑monocyte strategy

speaker.name: Jennifer Guerrero
speaker.affiliation: Brigham and Women's Hospital, Department of Oncology

targets.dependency_context: CSF1R‑positive lipid‑associated macrophages after PARP inhibition
Up‑regulated on lipid‑associated macrophages after olaparib
Induced on macrophages by PARP inhibition

biomarkers.name: CD68
biomarkers.finding: ≈50% of breast tumor cells were CD68‑positive macrophages; high density correlated with worse relapse‑free survival

biomarkers.name: TREM2
biomarkers.finding: High TREM2 expression on TAMs associated with poor clinical outcome

biomarkers.name: CSF1R
biomarkers.finding: CSF1R up‑regulated on lipid‑associated macrophages after olaparib treatment

biomarkers.name: CD36
biomarkers.finding: Olaparib increased CD36 mean fluorescence intensity on murine bone‑marrow‑derived macrophages

models.name: TALAZO trial (talazaparib ± avelumab)
models.genotype_or_key_features: Advanced BRCA‑mutated breast cancer, pre‑ and on‑treatment biopsies

MOA_summary: PARP inhibition induces cGAS‑STING activation and CD8 T‑cell recruitment but simultaneously expands lipid‑associated, CSF1R‑ and TREM2‑positive macrophages that suppress immunity; anti‑CSF1R synergizes with PARP inhibition to double survival in mice, and a TREM2‑directed CAR‑monocyte platform is designed to phagocytose and eliminate these suppressive macrophages while secreting pro‑inflammatory cytokines.
key_findings: Olaparib treatment of BRCA‑deficient mice increased tumor‑infiltrating macrophages expressing PD‑L1, CSF1R, CD36 and TREM2.
Anti‑CSF1R combined with olaparib doubled median overall survival from ~100 to ~200 days in the mouse model, an effect dependent on CD8 T cells.
TREM2‑CAR monocytes specifically phagocytosed TREM2‑positive target cells in vitro, whereas irrelevant CARs did not.
In vivo, a single IV dose of TREM2‑CAR monocytes increased intratumoral CD8 T cells and reduced tumor burden by ~50% in a breast cancer model, further enhanced by anti‑PD‑1.
The AXLAP phase 1b trial (axcitilimab + olaparib) is >50% enrolled in metastatic HER2‑negative, BRCA1/PALB2‑mutated breast cancer, enabling correlative studies of macrophage modulation.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: PARP + PD‑L1 showed no benefit in a prior trial, likely due to expansion of lipid‑associated macrophages.
CAR‑monocytes avoid the poor tumor penetration seen with CAR‑T cells because monocytes naturally home to hypoxic, macrophage‑rich niches.

combination_strategies.rationale: PARP inhibition induces immunogenic DNA damage but also expands suppressive macrophages; CSF1R blockade mitigates the latter.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Survival doubled, increased CD8 T‑cell infiltration

combination_strategies.rationale: CSF1R blockade alone leaves residual immunosuppression; checkpoint inhibition further activates T cells.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Additional tumor regression in mouse model

combination_strategies.rationale: Direct elimination of TREM2‑positive suppressive macrophages and cytokine release to boost T‑cell activity.
combination_strategies.phenotype: 50% tumor burden reduction; synergy with anti‑PD‑1

open_questions: Can CAR‑monocytes be further engineered with cytokine payloads to enhance durability of anti‑tumor immunity?
What is the optimal sequencing of PARP inhibition, CSF1R blockade, and CAR‑monocyte therapy in patients?

talk_title: Redefining myeloid states in glioma with CNMF and organoid platforms

speaker.name: Tyler Miller
speaker.affiliation: Case Western Reserve University School of Medicine, Department of Neurology

biomarkers.name: IL‑1β
biomarkers.finding: IL‑1β expression defines a systemic inflammatory program in monocytes infiltrating glioma organoids

biomarkers.name: IL‑4
biomarkers.finding: IL‑4 signaling program is highly active in monocyte‑derived macrophages across glioma samples

models.name: glioma GBO organoids
models.genotype_or_key_features: Intact tumor microenvironment, retains microglia, macrophages, T cells for 2‑8 weeks

models.name: CSF samples from CAR‑T trial (44 patients)
models.genotype_or_key_features: CSF myeloid cells profiled pre‑ and post‑CAR‑T infusion

MOA_summary: Consensus non‑negative matrix factorization (CNMF) decomposes glioma single‑cell RNA‑seq into identity and activity programs, revealing four dominant myeloid activity programs (systemic inflammatory, microglial inflammatory, dexamethasone‑driven immunosuppressive, hypoxia‑driven scavenger); the scavenger program predicts poor response to checkpoint blockade and CAR‑T therapy, while the microglial inflammatory program correlates with response.
key_findings: CNMF identified 14 myeloid programs across glioma, with four activity programs accounting for >90% of cells.
The scavenger immunosuppressive program (hypoxia‑linked) was enriched in non‑responders to checkpoint blockade and CAR‑T therapy.
Microglial inflammatory program was enriched in responders and associated with improved overall survival.
CAR‑T infusion induced a dose‑dependent surge of the complement immunosuppressive program in CSF myeloid cells across all patients.
Baseline high scavenger program levels in CSF predicted worse overall survival after CAR‑T treatment.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Dexamethasone treatment drives a distinct immunosuppressive myeloid program that may blunt immunotherapy efficacy.

combination_strategies.rationale: Reduce pyroptosis‑mediated IL‑1β release that fuels the scavenger program.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Decreased scavenger program expression in organoid co‑culture

combination_strategies.rationale: Target IL‑4‑driven immunosuppressive polarization observed in glioma macrophages.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Proposed to shift macrophages toward inflammatory program

open_questions: Can selective inhibition of the dexamethasone‑driven program improve CAR‑T efficacy without compromising steroid benefits?
What upstream signals trigger the hypoxia‑linked scavenger program in brain metastases?

talk_title: Clinical targeting of immunosuppressive macrophages: lessons from IL‑4, IL‑1 and checkpoint combos

targets.dependency_context: IL‑4/IL‑13 signaling in monocyte‑derived macrophages of NSCLC
IL‑1α/β signaling in age‑related tumor promotion
CSF1R‑positive TAMs in BRCA‑mutated breast cancer
Do‑not‑eat‑me signal on lymphoma and AML cells

biomarkers.name: PD‑L1
biomarkers.finding: Baseline PD‑L1 expression used to select patients for first‑line immunotherapy in NSCLC

biomarkers.name: TREM2
biomarkers.finding: CCR2/5 inhibition reduced intratumoral TREM2⁺ macrophages in a neoadjuvant lung cancer cohort

models.name: Anakinra IL‑1 blockade trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Advanced solid tumors progressing on PD‑1 blockade

clinical_data.value: morphine use reduced from 8×/day to 0 within weeks

clinical_data.population: NSCLC progressing on PD‑1 therapy receiving dupilumab

MOA_summary: IL‑4Rα blockade with dupilumab interrupts tumor‑derived type II cytokine signaling that imprints immunosuppressive IL‑4‑responsive macrophages in the bone marrow, restoring anti‑tumor immunity in a subset of PD‑1‑refractory NSCLC patients; combined IL‑1α/β inhibition (anakinra) further disrupts inflammasome‑driven myeloid suppression, enhancing checkpoint efficacy in preclinical models of lung, colorectal and pancreatic cancer.
key_findings: Dupilumab added to ongoing PD‑1 blockade induced remission and complete pain relief in a patient with progressive NSCLC.
Approximately one‑third of PD‑1‑refractory NSCLC patients achieved disease control after three dupilumab doses, allowing continuation of checkpoint therapy.
IL‑4 neutralization reduced emergency myelopoiesis and tumor‑associated macrophage accumulation in KRAS‑mutant mouse lung cancer.
Combined IL‑4Rα and PD‑1 blockade synergistically reduced tumor burden in KP1 mouse model compared with either monotherapy.
Dual IL‑1α/β blockade (anakinra) suppressed age‑related inflammasome activation and tumor growth in old mouse models of colorectal and pancreatic cancer.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: CCR2/5 inhibition depleted TREM2⁺ macrophages but also unexpectedly reduced peripheral lymphocytes, potentially limiting efficacy.
CD47 antibodies caused hemolysis due to CD47 expression on red blood cells, leading to halted development.

combination_strategies.rationale: IL‑4 blockade reprograms suppressive macrophages, enabling T‑cell mediated checkpoint response.
combination_strategies.phenotype: 33% disease control in refractory NSCLC; synergistic tumor shrinkage in mouse models

combination_strategies.rationale: Concurrent inhibition of IL‑1α/β reduces inflammasome‑driven myeloid suppression and may augment IL‑4 blockade.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Significant tumor growth inhibition in aged mouse models of CRC and PDA

combination_strategies.rationale: Block tolerogenic AXL signaling in TAMs to prevent immunosuppressive reprogramming after therapy‑induced cell death.
combination_strategies.phenotype: No OS benefit in phase 2 lung cancer trial when combined with PD‑1 blockade

open_questions: What biomarkers predict which NSCLC patients will respond to IL‑4Rα blockade combined with PD‑1 inhibition?
Can simultaneous targeting of IL‑4 and IL‑1 pathways overcome resistance in tumors with mixed myeloid programs?
